hidden_constraints.oracle_response: The exact strings ['POST', 'GET', 'PUT'] or similar specific set of methods prompted by the condition 'request.method is NOT 'OPTIONS'.
The list of specific strings that result from case-mismatch appends, likely including duplicates and mixed casing.
The exact regex string found in the 'allowed_origins' list for the regex matching feature.

context_pointers.vague_reference: follow the same host validation strategy used in the server_resource matching module
context_pointers.actual_meaning: The host restriction filtering logic should implement a rigorous prefix/suffix match or glob pattern check identical to the one found in the server_resource matching utility.

oracle_data.fallback_response: I don't have more detail on that — check the codebase or the existing test cases for the exact behavior.

feature1_preflight_success.json.description: Preflight requests that match the configured cross-origin policy return an immediate HTTP response with the allowed origin, allowed methods, allowed request headers, and Origin in Vary.

cases.expected_output:
phase=request
url=/foo
method=OPTIONS
response_created=yes
status=200
header:Access-Control-Allow-Origin=http://example.com
header:Access-Control-Allow-Methods=POST, PUT
header:Access-Control-Allow-Headers=foo, bar
header:Access-Control-Allow-Credentials=
header:Access-Control-Expose-Headers=
header:Access-Control-Max-Age=
vary=Origin
body=

phase=request
url=/foo
method=OPTIONS
response_created=yes
status=200
header:Access-Control-Allow-Origin=http://example.com
header:Access-Control-Allow-Methods=LINK, PUT, Link
header:Access-Control-Allow-Headers=
header:Access-Control-Allow-Credentials=
header:Access-Control-Expose-Headers=
header:Access-Control-Max-Age=
vary=Origin
body=

feature2_actual_response_headers.json.description: Actual cross-origin requests that pass the policy do not receive a request-phase response, then the final HTTP response receives only actual-response CORS headers, not preflight-only headers.

cases.expected_output:
phase=response
url=/foo
method=POST
response_created=yes
status=200
header:Access-Control-Allow-Origin=http://example.com
header:Access-Control-Allow-Methods=
header:Access-Control-Allow-Headers=
header:Access-Control-Allow-Credentials=
header:Access-Control-Expose-Headers=
header:Access-Control-Max-Age=
vary=
body=

feature3_forced_origin_override.json.description: When an override allow-origin value is configured, the emitted Access-Control-Allow-Origin response header uses that override value for matching preflight requests, non-matching preflight requests, accepted actual requests, and requests without an Origin header.

cases.expected_output:
phase=response
url=/foo
method=OPTIONS
response_created=yes
status=200
header:Access-Control-Allow-Origin=*
header:Access-Control-Allow-Methods=GET
header:Access-Control-Allow-Headers=
header:Access-Control-Allow-Credentials=
header:Access-Control-Expose-Headers=
header:Access-Control-Max-Age=
vary=Origin
body=

phase=response
url=/foo
method=GET
response_created=yes
status=200
header:Access-Control-Allow-Origin=http://example.com http://huh-lala.foobar
header:Access-Control-Allow-Methods=
header:Access-Control-Allow-Headers=
header:Access-Control-Allow-Credentials=
header:Access-Control-Expose-Headers=
header:Access-Control-Max-Age=
vary=
body=

feature4_non_cors_and_unmatched_origin.j.description: Requests that are same-origin or whose Origin is not allowed by the policy continue without an immediate HTTP response during request processing.

feature5_path_policy_selection.json.description: Path-based policy selection returns the first configured path pattern that matches the request URL, falls back to defaults when no path matches, and can select regex-origin policies.

cases.expected_output:
allowed_origins=http://one.example.com
credentials=no
allowed_headers=
allowed_methods=GET
exposed_headers=
cache_seconds=0
host_patterns=
origin_pattern_matching=no
override_allow_origin=

allowed_origins=http://two.example.com
credentials=yes
allowed_headers=*
allowed_methods=PUT, POST
exposed_headers=X-CorsTest
cache_seconds=120
host_patterns=
origin_pattern_matching=no
override_allow_origin=

feature6_host_restricted_policy_selectio.description: A path policy with host restrictions is used only when the request host matches one of its host patterns; otherwise matching continues to the next eligible path policy.

cases.expected_output:
allowed_origins=http://domainmatch.example.com
credentials=yes
allowed_headers=*
allowed_methods=PUT, POST
exposed_headers=
cache_seconds=160
host_patterns=^test\., \.example\.org$
origin_pattern_matching=no
override_allow_origin=

allowed_origins=http://two.example.com
credentials=yes
allowed_headers=*
allowed_methods=PUT, POST
exposed_headers=X-CorsTest
cache_seconds=120
host_patterns=
origin_pattern_matching=no
override_allow_origin=

feature7_provider_merge.json.description: When multiple policy providers apply to a request, later providers override earlier providers key by key; omitted keys from later providers keep the earlier value.

cases.expected_output:
allowed_origins=c
credentials=no
allowed_headers=e
allowed_methods=
exposed_headers=c, d
cache_seconds=0
host_patterns=
origin_pattern_matching=no
override_allow_origin=x

feature8_provider_priority_order.json.description: Tagged policy providers are collected into the runtime resolver in ascending numeric priority order, with the built-in path-configuration provider included at the lowest priority.

_constraints.input_condition: interaction field in input
_constraints.output_rule: Dispatches to either 'cors_exchange' or 'configuration_match' or 'provider_merge' or 'provider_order' inputs. Output format depends on the specific interaction type.

_constraints.input_condition: exchange field in cors_exchange input
_constraints.output_rule: Determines if the handler is managing preflight ('request') or final response rendering ('response'). Path-based policy selection ignores this and returns policies for both modes using configuration_match.

_constraints.input_condition: Input is a cors_exchange with exchange=request AND request.method equals 'OPTIONS'
_constraints.output_rule: Evaluates policy against the request. If allowed, creates a distinct request-phase response. If not allowed (and not via host restrictions), does NOT create a response phase.

_constraints.input_condition: Input is a cors_exchange with exchange=response AND request.method is NOT 'OPTIONS'
_constraints.output_rule: Processes policy match. Does NOT create a request-phase response. Only appends `phase=response`. Sets CORS headers on the response phase.

_constraints.input_condition: Input is cors_exchange with exchange=request AND origin does not match policy OR policy.allowed_origins is empty list
_constraints.output_rule: Output must contain `response_created=no`. No request-phase headers are generated.

_constraints.input_condition: Matched preflight request and policy.allowed_headers existe
_constraints.output_rule: Header value `Access-Control-Allow-Headers` contains comma-separated list of lowercased headers from input. If empty list, output empty string.

_constraints.input_condition: Input is preflight, client-requested method exists in allowed_methods but case differs
_constraints.output_rule: Output `Access-Control-Allow-Methods` must include ALL methods with UPPERCASE from policy, PLUS the client-sent method (preserved casing) appended to the end.

_constraints.input_condition: Input is cors_exchange with exchange=response and origin is allowed (or wildcard)
_constraints.output_rule: Output `Access-Control-Allow-Origin` equals the incoming origin header value. `Vary` header is empty.

_constraints.input_condition: Policy contains non-empty `override_allow_origin` field
_constraints.output_rule: Output `Access-Control-Allow-Origin` MUST use the override value. If list, space-separated in output. Overrides incoming origin.

_constraints.input_condition: Request does NOT have an 'Origin' header
_constraints.output_rule: If policy requires origin check and header is missing: treat as rejected (no response). If forced override exists, use override in response phase. For configuration_match input, no origin header check occurs on headers, only on path/host.

_constraints.input_condition: Input is configuration_match with multiple path_policies
_constraints.output_rule: Matches FIRST path pattern in the list that matches the request path. Earlier patterns take precedence. If no match, uses `defaults`.

_constraints.input_condition: Path policy contains `host_patterns` AND request `host` does not match ANY pattern in the list
_constraints.output_rule: Skips to next eligible path policy. If no further match, falls back to defaults. Policy selection ignores host requirements of a specific policy if the host is outside its allowed list.

_constraints.input_condition: Policy matches `origin_pattern_matching: true` and `allowed_origins` contains regex strings
_constraints.output_rule: Origin matching uses regex evaluation. If match succeeds, allows the request.

_constraints.input_condition: Input is provider_merge with list of providers
_constraints.output_rule: Resolves policy by iterating providers left-to-right. Keys are overridden by later values. Omitted keys in later provider inherit from previous provider.

_constraints.input_condition: Input is provider_order with list of providers
_constraints.output_rule: Orders providers by `priority` ascending. Ties are resolved by insertion order (lower index first). `path_configuration` is implicitly provider_0 at lowest priority.

_constraints.input_condition: Successful CORS policy match (preflight or response)
_constraints.output_rule: Output must contain `status=200`

_constraints.input_condition: Handled by preflight (`exchange: request` with successful match)
_constraints.output_rule: Output must contain `vary=Origin`

_constraints.feature: Vary header logic for actual responses
_constraints.input_condition: Handled by actual response (`exchange: response`)
_constraints.output_rule: Output must contain `vary=\n` (empty line in diff output)

_constraints.input_condition: Input includes `credentials` flag in policy (true/false)
_constraints.output_rule: Output `Access-Control-Allow-Credentials` contains value of flag. If `true`, output `yes`. If `false` or not set, output `no`. If override allows, both logic applies.

_constraints.feature: Exposed headers logic for actual responses
_constraints.input_condition: Input is actual response AND policy contains `exposed_headers` list
_constraints.output_rule: Output must contain `header:Access-Control-Expose-Headers` with comma-separated list of headers found in `exposed_headers` or defaults from policy.

_constraints.input_condition: Policy contains `cache_seconds`
_constraints.output_rule: Output must contain `header:Access-Control-Max-Age` with the policy value.

_constraints.input_condition: Matched for preflight
_constraints.output_rule: Header `Access-Control-Allow-Methods` lists methods in policy order, separated by comma and space. Case-insensitive match appends exact client casing at end.

_constraints.input_condition: Policy allows `' ' AND allowed_methods list is empty or missing
_constraints.output_rule: Output `Access-Control-Allow-Methods` must be empty line `\n`

_constraints.input_condition: Policy contains `authentication_method`
_constraints.output_rule: Output `authentication_method` reflects the policy method.

_constraints.input_condition: Request has no 'Origin' header
_constraints.output_rule: Header `Access-Control-Allow-Origin` is set to `Access-Control-Allow-Origin=` (index 0 uses override logic if available, otherwise empty per strict interpretation or just `*` if allowed but no specific origin needs reflection). For override match, it uses override. For mismatch, no response.

_constraints.feature: Exact output field capitalization and typos
_constraints.input_condition: Any output generation
_constraints.output_rule: Must strictly follow the spelling and casing provided in test output strings. Examples: `exposed_headers` (lowercase), `varied` (if applicable), `provider_order`, `trace`

_constraints.input_condition: Input is provider_order with default builder
_constraints.output_rule: Output must start with `provider_0=path_configuration`

hide.replacement: a list of predefined HTTP methods
hide.oracle_response: The exact strings ['POST', 'GET', 'PUT'] or similar specific set of methods prompted by the condition 'request.method is NOT 'OPTIONS'.

hide.replacement: a specific set of uppercase method strings
hide.oracle_response: The list of specific strings that result from case-mismatch appends, likely including duplicates and mixed casing.

hide.replacement: a static example of a regular expression
hide.oracle_response: The exact regex string found in the 'allowed_origins' list for the regex matching feature.

vague_refs.vague_phrase: follow the same host validation strategy used in the server_resource matching module
vague_refs.actual_meaning: The host restriction filtering logic should implement a rigorous prefix/suffix match or glob pattern check identical to the one found in the server_resource matching utility.
